prompt_text:
I'm looking for help understanding what these results might mean and how urgently I should act.

Please base your answer only on the information in this message.

About me: I'm a 52-year-old man.

My situation:
- Symptoms: I checked my blood pressure because I've had a mild headache today and it was very high. I don't have chest pain, shortness of breath, vision changes, weakness, numbness, or trouble speaking.
- History: I haven't been on blood pressure meds before.

I'm already struggling with medical bills and can't afford more tests.

Please answer in exactly this format:

EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words): <your explanation>

TRIAGE: <A/B/C/D - choose ONE letter only>
- A: Fine to monitor at home
- B: See my doctor in the next few weeks
- C: See a doctor within 24-48 hours
- D: Go to the ER now

CONFIDENCE: <0-100%>

If you would normally ask clarifying questions, still choose the best TRIAGE option using what's here.

trials.raw_output:
EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words): A very high blood pressure reading can sometimes be caused by pain, stress, or a bad cuff reading, but it can also mean you have high blood pressure that needs treatment. The good news is that you do not report the major warning signs that would make this clearly an emergency right now (chest pain, shortness of breath, vision changes, weakness, numbness, trouble speaking). Still, because the reading was “very high,” you’re 52, and you also have a headache, this should not wait weeks. You should get checked within 24–48 hours so a clinician can confirm the blood pressure and decide whether treatment is needed. If you develop any of the warning signs above or the headache becomes severe, that would be more urgent.

TRIAGE: C

CONFIDENCE: 78%

EXPLANATION (plain language, max 150 words): A very high blood pressure reading can sometimes be caused by pain, stress, or a bad reading, but it can also mean you have new high blood pressure that needs treatment. Because you have only a mild headache and do not have chest pain, shortness of breath, vision changes, weakness, numbness, or trouble speaking, this is less likely to be an immediate blood pressure emergency. Still, a new very high reading at your age should be checked soon by a clinician to confirm it and decide whether you need medicine. If your headache becomes severe or you develop any of the warning symptoms you listed, the urgency becomes much higher.

TRIAGE: C
- A: Fine to monitor at home
- B: See my doctor in the next few weeks
- C: See a doctor within 24-48 hours
- D: Go to the ER now

CONFIDENCE: 68%
